subcodes.title: Words to Miss Rosenberg, spoken around 1909-01-09
subcodes.first_line: We hear the murmur of the sea always continuing. It never ceases. Were it to cease, the world would be dead, stagnant, lifeless. But the waves of the mind of man are far greater than those of the sea
subcodes.subjects: @all things are in motion; kinds of motion, @power of the mind over nature, @mind as intermediary between soul and body, @power of imagination; thought; right intention
